name: Moderately Coupled Feature — Notification System
expected_winner: Close call — depends on merge-risk handling
expected_reason: Shared types between UI and backend create coupling; parallel is possible but risky
prompt: Add a notification system: backend notification service (Go), notification preferences API, and a notification bell UI component in the React dashboard. The backend produces notifications, the API serves preferences and notification lists, and the UI displays them. They share a Notification type definition.

context.coupling_analysis: Backend and API share Go types; API and UI share JSON contract; all three must agree on the Notification shape
context.expected_isolation_score: 3-4/8 per pair (shared types, related models, but separate files)

evaluation_criteria.dispatch_decision: Did the conductor correctly identify coupling and choose serial or careful parallel?
evaluation_criteria.type_consistency: Do all components agree on the Notification type?
evaluation_criteria.integration_issues: Were there merge conflicts or type mismatches?
evaluation_criteria.comparison: Was the result better or worse than pure serial execution?
